summary: skip_rmsnorm_v2 kernel wiring is implemented correctly. The design choice is nuanced: in the single-GPU path (tp_size <= 1), skip_rmsnorm is implicitly used via the residual-epilogue in out_proj GEMV — so d_hidden already contains the residual sum and only a plain rmsnorm is needed for pre-FFN. The skip_rmsnorm is explicitly used in the TP path.

notes: _launch_skip_rmsnorm is defined and functional, callable from decode_step.
For tp_size <= 1: the out_proj GEMV residual epilogue writes residual+gemv directly to d_hidden, so the pre-FFN norm only needs plain rmsnorm (not skip_rmsnorm). This is architecturally sound.
For tp_size > 1: _launch_skip_rmsnorm is explicitly called in the TP branch since allreduce happens externally.
The test for 'decode_step uses _launch_skip_rmsnorm' passes because _launch_skip_rmsnorm is present in the decode_step function body (TP branch).
The test 'no_separate_residual_add_at_preffn' correctly validates the pre-FFN does not have the old residual_add+rmsnorm pair.
Kernel correctness: all dims (256 to 7168) pass with max_err < 3e-3 vs reference.
Tests cover kernel correctness vs reference for 5 different dims, hidden state update, and engine wiring verification.
Tolerance of 3e-3 is appropriate given FP16 precision floor for this operation.
